message:
feat(engine): security hardening, interpreter prototype chain, IFC label ordering, parser depth guard, and TS strip_type_annotations rewrite

Baseline interpreter (baseline_interpreter.rs):
- Add prototype_chain_get() for property lookups that walk the prototype
  chain with cycle detection and MAX_PROTOTYPE_CHAIN_DEPTH guard, used
  by GetProperty instead of flat single-object lookup
- Add JS-spec numeric coercion for Add/Sub/Mul/Div: bool and null now
  coerce to numbers (true=1, false=0, null=0) instead of TypeError
- Update existing type-error tests to verify coercion semantics

IFC artifacts (ifc_artifacts.rs):
- Replace derived Ord on Label enum with manual impl based on level()
  to ensure security-correct ordering — derived Ord ordered by
  discriminant, placing Custom(level=1) above TopSecret(level=4)

Extension registry (extension_registry.rs):
- Add PublisherAlreadyExists error: reject duplicate publisher
  registration to prevent re-registration after revocation
- Enforce monotonic tick advancement (ignore backward ticks)
- Exclude revoked publishers' packages from search results
- Fail-closed is_package_revoked(): unknown packages now return true
  instead of false
- Verify signatures against registry's publisher key, not manifest's
  embedded key, to detect key tampering
- Update verify_package test expectations

Parser (parser.rs):
- Add statement_depth tracking with budget-based recursion limit to
  prevent stack overflow from deeply nested statements
- Handle backtick in split_statement_segments and split_var_declarator_
  segments to prevent template literal contents from splitting
- Fix template literal parsing to advance by full UTF-8 codepoints
  instead of single bytes, preventing mid-codepoint splits on
  multi-byte characters and escaped multi-byte characters

TS normalization (ts_normalization.rs):
- Rewrite strip_type_annotations from char-based to byte-indexed
  scanner with proper Ctx state machine tracking single quotes, double
  quotes, template literals, line comments, and block comments —
  preventing type annotation stripping inside string/comment contexts

Regime shift detector (regime_shift_detector.rs):
- Use i128 intermediaries in CUSUM update to avoid silent wrapping
  when u64 values exceed i64::MAX
- Reset ticks_since_last_shift during cooldown suppression so
  auto-adapt does not assume stability during suppressed shifts
- Recompute config_hash from current state instead of cached value

Parallel parser: Use u128 intermediate in throughput computation to
avoid overflow for inputs > ~18 MB

Capability witness: Supersede previous active witness on re-registration

Sim scheduler: Break early when all remaining events exceed max_ticks

Other: flow_lattice saturating_add + doc comment, semantic_transport
saturating_sub, regret_bounded_router negative variance clamp,
execution_orchestrator expect() instead of unwrap_or_default()

Tests: 1,928 lines of new integration test coverage across 6 test files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1025,7 +1025,10 @@ impl SemanticTransportAnalyzer {
                     &format!(
                         "Unchanged verdict for '{}' but {}/{} invariants unverified.",
                         entry.fragment_name,
-                        entry.required_invariants.len() - entry.verified_invariants.len(),
+                        entry
+                            .required_invariants
+                            .len()
+                            .saturating_sub(entry.verified_invariants.len()),
                         entry.required_invariants.len(),
                     ),
                     400_000, // Medium risk.
